Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine and marked proteinuria with high microalbumin/creatinine ratio, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, and bilirubin present. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.
